text.title: Platform Engineering Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules for the IDP spec. Each rule has empirical roots in Spotify / Humanitec / CNCF platform engineering data.

rule.statement: Platform-orchestration logic (provisioning, quotas, RBAC, multi-env mapping) MUST be working before any developer-portal UI is built.
rule.rationale: Portal-first creates the illusion of progress while the hard problem — service composition, quota enforcement, identity model — remains unsolved. The portal then leaks orchestration gaps to every user simultaneously.

rule.statement: Ship exactly ONE golden path in v1. Generalise to multiple paths only after the first one has ≥10 services using it and ≥3 months of feedback.
rule.rationale: Multiple early paths spread the platform team thin; each path gets shallow investment and teams diverge anyway. The rule of three is empirical: deep investment in one path beats shallow investment in five.

rule.statement: Golden-path templates MUST be semver-versioned (path-v1.2.3). Updates MUST be opt-in via PR; the platform NEVER silently mutates downstream repos.
rule.rationale: Silent template updates break consumers without warning, eroding trust and causing 'we have to escape the platform' anti-bodies.

rule.statement: The platform team's dashboard MUST display DORA (Deployment Frequency, Lead Time, MTTR, Change Failure Rate) AND platform-specific metrics (self-service adoption rate, TTFD for new devs, ticket trend, NPS).

rationale.source: Google DORA report, Humanitec Platform Maturity Model

rule.rationale: Without metrics the platform becomes invisible — accountable for nothing. DORA alone misses the platform-specific signal (adoption); platform metrics alone miss the business outcome (delivery velocity).

rule.statement: RBAC, quota, audit-log, and identity model MUST treat AI agents as first-class principals (own identity, own scope, own quota) — not as a superuser running on behalf of a human.
rule.rationale: Conflating agent + human identity makes incidents un-debuggable ('who pushed this?') and creates a privilege-escalation surface where any human's session token is also an AI's token.

rule.statement: Spec MUST contain an `non_goals_year1` block naming what the platform explicitly will NOT do this year.

rationale.source: Internal platform-team retros across multiple orgs

rule.rationale: Without explicit non-goals, every team asks for everything, the platform team over-commits, and confidence collapses when the inevitable cuts happen.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
